comments.body:
> Thanks for the PR, a couple of comments on how to improve this PR
> 
> 
> Tests, would you mind writing a unit test for inspect in `podman/tests/unit/test_volume.py
> 
> 
> Please sign your contribution with `git commit -s` and please add `Fixes: <link to GH issue>` for future convenience :)

1. Created one unit test 'test_inspect' 
2.  Sure. Next commit is signed and more appropriate commit message. I'm sorry for basic mistakes, but it is my first contribution to open project.

> but it is my first contribution to open project.

Welcome! you are welcome to ping me if you need further information on how to contribute to podman-py :)

> 1. would suggest you to squash both commits into one, then si

Seems like managed to squash both commits without braking repo.
Also cleaned up code with linter
Thanks for patience and guidance. Really appreciate this

> Thanks, that's awesome. I should be more precise about how we craft commits which it's usually with a title, description if necessary (in your case I think only title is enough), references and signature, so maybe you could polish that a little bit :)

Changed commit message. I promise my next contribution will not be such problematic ;)

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *inknos*, *rkozlo* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman-py). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman-py/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [inknos] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
